right: working or studying very late at night
explanation: <Burn the midnight oil> means to work late into the night.

left: the ball is in your court
right: it's your turn to make a decision or take action
explanation: <The ball is in your court> means it's up to you to act next.

left: kill two birds with one stone
right: achieve two things with a single action
explanation: <Kill two birds with one stone> means to accomplish two goals at once.

right: not making a decision between two options
explanation: <Sit on the fence> means to be undecided or neutral.

right: become nervous and afraid to do something planned
explanation: <Get cold feet> means to lose confidence before doing something.

right: be too late to take advantage of an opportunity
explanation: <Miss the boat> means to miss a chance or opportunity.

right: something bad that turns out to have a good result
explanation: <A blessing in disguise> means a misfortune that leads to something good.

right: avoid talking about the main topic directly
explanation: <Beat around the bush> means to avoid getting to the point.

right: make a bad situation even worse
explanation: <Add insult to injury> means to worsen an already bad situation.

right: do something in the cheapest or easiest way
explanation: <Cut corners> means to save time or money by doing things less carefully.

right: make more effort than is expected
explanation: <Go the extra mile> means to do more than what is required.

right: agree with someone completely
explanation: <See eye to eye> means to have the same opinion as someone.

left: hit the nail on the head
right: describe exactly what is causing a problem
explanation: <Hit the nail on the head> means to be exactly right about something.

right: avoid interfering with a situation that could cause trouble
explanation: <Let sleeping dogs lie> means to leave a problem alone to avoid making it worse.

left: bite off more than you can chew
right: take on a task that is too big for you
explanation: <Bite off more than you can chew> means to try to do too much.

right: cost too much money
explanation: <Break the bank> means something is very expensive.

right: joke with someone or tease them
explanation: <Pull someone's leg> means to joke with them in a playful way.

right: without being noticed or attracting attention
explanation: <Under the radar> means to go unnoticed.

right: a small visible part of a much larger problem
explanation: <The tip of the iceberg> means only a small part of a bigger issue is visible.

right: give up or admit defeat
explanation: <Throw in the towel> means to stop trying and accept failure.
